title: No Paper / Paper Feed Error
shortDescription: No paper loaded in the rear tray or paper cassette, or paper failed to feed.
fullDescription: Support Code 1000 indicates the printer cannot detect paper in the selected paper source or the paper failed to feed into the printer. The paper tray may be empty, the paper may be loaded incorrectly, or the feed rollers may be dirty or worn.

causes.cause: Paper not loaded properly or guides misaligned
Paper too thick, thin, or curled for the feed mechanism

symptoms: Support Code 1000 on printer LCD or computer
Feed rollers spin but paper does not advance
Print job pauses waiting for paper

fixSteps.title: Load Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear tray or pull out the paper cassette. Load a stack of paper and adjust the guides to fit snugly against the edges.

fixSteps.title: Fan and Align Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Fan the paper stack to separate sheets and prevent multi-feeds. Align the edges evenly before loading.

fixSteps.title: Clean Feed Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: From the printer maintenance menu, run the Roller Cleaning function. This feeds a blank page through to clean the rollers. Run 2-3 times if needed.

fixSteps.title: Manual Roller Cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: If the maintenance function does not help, dampen a lint-free cloth with water and manually clean the gray rubber feed rollers visible inside the rear tray area.

whenToCallPro: If feed rollers are physically worn smooth and roller cleaning does not help, the rollers may need professional replacement.

faq.q: How much paper should I load?
faq.a: Load paper up to the maximum line marked on the tray guide. Do not exceed this as overfilling causes jams and feed failures.

faq.q: Can I use any paper brand?
faq.a: Canon recommends using paper that meets the weight and size specifications in your printer manual. Most standard 75-90 g/m2 copy paper works well.

faq.q: How often should I clean the rollers?
faq.a: Run the roller cleaning maintenance function every 1-2 months, or whenever you notice paper feeding issues.

title: Cover Open
shortDescription: The printer's top cover or scanning unit is open.
fullDescription: Support Code 1200 indicates the top cover or scanning unit is not fully closed. The printer pauses all operations when covers are open for safety and to prevent ink or toner contamination.

fixSteps.title: Close the Cover
fixSteps.instruction: Press down firmly on the top cover until it clicks into the closed position.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Lift the cover and check for paper, packaging material, or objects preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Verify Cartridge Seating
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all ink cartridges are fully pushed down into their slots. A raised cartridge can prevent the cover from closing.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists with the cover closed, turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on.

whenToCallPro: Only if the cover sensor or latch mechanism is physically broken.

faq.q: Why does the cover not close fully?
faq.a: Most commonly, a cartridge is not fully seated, or a piece of paper or packaging is in the way. Inspect the area carefully.

faq.q: Can a cracked cover cause this?
faq.a: If the cover is cracked or warped, it may not engage the sensor. You may need a replacement cover assembly.

faq.q: Is it safe to open the cover while printing?
faq.a: The printer will stop immediately when the cover opens. This is a safety feature. However, avoid opening mid-print as it can cause ink smearing on the printhead or paper path.

title: Paper Jam
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer.
fullDescription: Support Code 1300 is the primary paper jam error for Canon printers. Paper is stuck inside the print mechanism and must be removed before printing can resume. The jam can be in the feed area, print zone, or output area.

causes.cause: Paper loaded unevenly or skewed in tray
Too many sheets loaded in tray

symptoms: Grinding or crunching noise during feed

fixSteps.title: Turn Off Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Press the power button to turn off the printer before removing jammed paper.

fixSteps.title: Remove Paper from Rear
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover or transport unit. Slowly pull jammed paper straight out. Do not pull at an angle to avoid tearing.

fixSteps.title: Check Front and Output
fixSteps.instruction: Check the output tray area for partially ejected paper. Also lift the scanning unit to check for paper in the print zone.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Scraps
fixSteps.instruction: Use a flashlight to look for small torn paper fragments inside the printer. Even small pieces can cause repeated jams.

whenToCallPro: If jams recur constantly after clearing and proper paper loading, internal rollers or guides may need professional replacement.

faq.q: Should I pull jammed paper forward or backward?
faq.a: Pull in the direction the paper was traveling. For rear jams, pull straight back through the rear cover. For front jams, pull forward through the output area.

faq.q: What if the paper tears inside?
faq.a: Use a flashlight to locate the torn fragment. You may need to open the rear transport unit to access it. Do not use metal tools that could damage the rollers.

faq.q: Why does my printer jam with photo paper?
faq.a: Photo paper is thicker and coated. Load fewer sheets (5-10 at a time) and ensure the media type is set correctly in the print driver.

title: Paper Jam in Transport Unit
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the rear transport unit or duplex area.
fullDescription: Support Code 1303 specifically identifies a paper jam in the rear transport unit. This area handles paper transport from the tray through the print zone. On printers with automatic duplexing, this also covers the duplex paper path.

causes.cause: Paper stuck in rear transport path
Transport unit not properly seated after previous jam clearance

symptoms: Paper visible in rear of printer
Rear transport unit feels restricted when opening

fixSteps.title: Open Rear Transport Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Push the release lever on the rear of the printer and pull out the transport unit or rear cover.

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pull any visible paper straight out of the transport unit. Check both sides of the unit for paper scraps.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Duplex Path
fixSteps.instruction: If your printer has auto-duplex, check inside the duplex feed path for jammed paper. The path is visible with the transport unit removed.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Transport Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Push the transport unit back in firmly until it clicks into place. An improperly seated unit causes repeated jams.

whenToCallPro: If the transport unit mechanism is physically damaged or the duplex feed path cannot be cleared.

faq.q: Where is the transport unit?
faq.a: The transport unit is at the rear of the printer. It has a release lever or button that allows you to pull it out for jam clearance.

faq.q: Can I still print if the transport unit is removed?
faq.a: No, the printer will not operate with the transport unit removed. It must be properly seated for printing to resume.

faq.q: Why do I get jams only during duplex printing?
faq.a: The paper must reverse direction for the second side, increasing jam risk. Use paper within 60-105 g/m2 for reliable duplexing.

title: Paper Jam in Output Area
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the paper output slot or output tray area.
fullDescription: Support Code 1304 indicates paper is stuck in the output area of the printer, typically visible in the output tray slot. The paper has passed through the print zone but stalled before being fully ejected.

causes.cause: Output tray full blocking paper exit
Paper curling from print process catching on exit
Thick media stalling at output slot

symptoms: Paper partially sticking out of output slot
Printed page with smeared or damaged print
Error occurs at end of print job

fixSteps.title: Clear Output Tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all printed pages from the output tray to make room for new pages to exit.

fixSteps.title: Pull Jammed Paper Out
fixSteps.instruction: Gently pull the jammed page straight out from the output slot. Hold both edges to avoid tearing.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Inside
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanning unit and check for paper stuck just before the output area.

fixSteps.title: Clean Output Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Run the roller cleaning utility from the printer maintenance menu to clean the output transport rollers.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. Only if the output mechanism is physically damaged.

faq.q: How many pages can the output tray hold?
faq.a: Most Canon PIXMA printers hold 50-75 pages in the output tray. Enterprise MAXIFY models may hold more. Remove pages regularly during large print jobs.

faq.q: Why do my prints curl at the output?
faq.a: Paper curl is caused by heat and moisture during printing. It is worse with thin paper and high humidity. Using heavier paper (80+ g/m2) reduces curl.

faq.q: Can I extend the output tray?
faq.a: Most Canon printers have an extendable output tray with a stopper. Pull it out to catch pages and prevent them from falling off.

title: Print Head Not Installed
shortDescription: The print head is not detected or not properly installed.
fullDescription: Support Code 1401 indicates the printer cannot detect the print head. On Canon PIXMA printers, the print head is a separate user-replaceable component that holds the ink cartridges. It may be improperly installed, have dirty contacts, or have failed.

causes.cause: Print head not fully seated in carriage

symptoms: Error appears after cartridge or print head change

fixSteps.title: Reseat Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top cover, remove all ink cartridges, lift the print head lock lever, remove the print head, wait 10 seconds, then reinstall it firmly and lock the lever.

fixSteps.title: Clean Print Head Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the print head and clean the gold electrical contacts on the print head and inside the carriage with a lint-free cloth dampened with distilled water.

fixSteps.title: Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer on, unplug the power cord. Wait 2 minutes. Reconnect and power on.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, the print head may have failed. Order a replacement print head for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the carriage contacts are damaged and a new print head is also not detected.

faq.q: Where do I buy a Canon print head?
faq.a: Canon print heads are available from Canon's online store, authorized dealers, and some electronics retailers. Search for your specific print head model number.

faq.q: Can I use the printer without a print head?
faq.a: No, the print head is essential for printing. The printer cannot function without a properly installed print head.

faq.q: How long does a Canon print head last?
faq.a: Under normal use, Canon print heads can last several years. Infrequent printing (less than once a month) can cause nozzle clogging that shortens lifespan.

title: Print Head Not Compatible
shortDescription: The installed print head is not compatible with this printer model.
fullDescription: Support Code 1403 indicates the installed print head is not the correct type for this printer model. Canon uses different print heads for different printer families, and they are not interchangeable.

causes.cause: Print head from different Canon printer family

symptoms: Printer rejects newly installed print head
Error immediately after print head installation

fixSteps.title: Verify Print Head Compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Check your printer's user manual or Canon's website for the correct print head part number for your specific model.

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the print head contacts in case the error is a communication issue rather than actual incompatibility.

fixSteps.title: Install Correct Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If the wrong print head is installed, remove it and install the correct one for your model.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After installing the correct print head, power off and on to allow proper initialization.

whenToCallPro: This is typically a user error that does not require professional service.

faq.q: How do I find the right print head for my Canon?
faq.a: The correct print head number is listed in your printer manual, on Canon's support site, or on the label inside the cartridge access area.

faq.q: Are Canon print heads interchangeable between models?
faq.a: No, Canon print heads are specific to printer families. Even visually similar heads from different series will not work.

faq.q: Can aftermarket print heads cause this error?
faq.a: Yes, non-Canon print heads may not have compatible identification chips and can trigger the 1403 error.

title: Print Head Error / Unrecognized
shortDescription: The print head cannot be recognized or has an error.
fullDescription: Support Code 1405 indicates a general print head recognition error. The printer detects a print head is present but cannot properly communicate with it. This is more severe than 1401 (not installed) and may indicate a hardware fault.

causes.cause: Print head chip or circuit failure
Printer firmware issue with print head recognition
Moisture or ink contamination on contacts

symptoms: Print head previously working now rejected
Multiple print heads showing same error

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts Thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all cartridges and the print head. Clean both the print head contacts and carriage contacts with isopropyl alcohol on a lint-free cloth. Allow to dry completely.

fixSteps.title: Check for Ink Contamination
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the contact areas for ink that may have leaked onto the electrical contacts. Clean thoroughly if ink is present.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: If possible, update the printer firmware through Canon's IJ Printer Utility or the printer menu. Firmware updates can fix recognition issues.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after thorough cleaning and firmware update, the print head has failed and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If a new print head also shows 1405, the carriage electronics need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: Is 1405 more serious than 1401?
faq.a: Yes, 1401 usually means the head is not installed. 1405 means the head is detected but has a communication error, which more often indicates hardware failure.

faq.q: Can ink spills cause this error?
faq.a: Yes, ink that reaches the electrical contacts can cause short circuits or corrosion, leading to communication failures. Thorough cleaning may resolve it.

faq.q: Should I try a different print head before buying a new one?
faq.a: If possible, yes. Try a known-working print head from the same model to determine if the issue is the head or the printer's carriage contacts.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Recognized
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges cannot be recognized by the printer.
fullDescription: Support Code 1410 indicates one or more ink cartridges are not being recognized. The cartridge chip cannot communicate with the printer, either due to dirty contacts, an incompatible cartridge, or a defective chip.

causes.cause: Cartridge not fully inserted into slot

symptoms: Printer will not start print job

fixSteps.title: Reseat the Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the identified cartridge and reinstall it firmly until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean Cartridge Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and clean the chip contacts with a lint-free cloth. Also clean the contacts inside the print head where the cartridge connects.

fixSteps.title: Verify Cartridge Compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the cartridge number matches what your printer requires. Check the label inside the cartridge access area.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If the same slot keeps rejecting cartridges, try a different cartridge. If a different cartridge works, the original is defective.

whenToCallPro: Not typically needed. This is a cartridge or contact issue.

faq.q: Can I use third-party cartridges with my Canon?
faq.a: Third-party cartridges may work but can trigger recognition errors if their chips are not compatible. Canon-original cartridges provide the most reliable recognition.

faq.q: Does removing the protective tape help?
faq.a: Ensure you removed the orange protective tab and any tape from the cartridge before installation. Tape covering the contacts prevents recognition.

faq.q: Why does my cartridge work intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent recognition usually indicates dirty or corroded contacts. Clean both the cartridge and printer-side contacts thoroughly.

title: Ink Absorber Almost Full
shortDescription: The ink absorber (waste ink pad) is almost full.
fullDescription: Support Code 1700 is a warning that the ink absorber pads inside the printer are approaching capacity. These pads collect waste ink from printhead cleaning cycles and borderless printing. The printer can continue operating but will eventually require service when the absorber is completely full (error 5B00).

causes.cause: Accumulated waste ink from cleaning cycles

symptoms: Warning appears during startup or after cleaning

fixSteps.title: Acknowledge Warning
fixSteps.instruction: Press OK or Resume to dismiss the warning and continue printing. This is a warning, not an error that stops the printer.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Unnecessary Cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid excessive manual printhead cleaning. Only clean when print quality visibly deteriorates. Each cleaning cycle uses ink and fills the absorber.

fixSteps.title: Plan for Service
fixSteps.instruction: Start planning to have the printer serviced or the ink absorber replaced. Contact Canon support or an authorized service center.

fixSteps.title: Reset Absorber Counter (Advanced)
fixSteps.instruction: For experienced users, the Canon Service Tool can reset the waste ink counter after replacing or cleaning the ink absorber pads. This is not officially supported by Canon.

whenToCallPro: When the absorber becomes completely full (error 5B00), the printer will stop and require professional service or absorber replacement.

faq.q: Can I continue printing with this warning?
faq.a: Yes, the 1700 warning does not stop printing. Press OK to continue. The printer will stop only when the absorber is completely full (error 5B00/5B01).

faq.q: How long until the absorber is full?
faq.a: This depends on usage, especially printhead cleaning frequency. It could be weeks to months after the first warning.

faq.q: Can I replace the ink absorber myself?
faq.a: Technically yes, but it requires disassembling the printer and resetting the counter with service software. Canon recommends professional service.

title: Ink Absorber Almost Full (Secondary)
shortDescription: The secondary ink absorber pad is almost full.
fullDescription: Support Code 1701 indicates the secondary or auxiliary ink absorber is approaching capacity. Some Canon printers have multiple absorber pads for different waste ink collection points. This warning functions identically to 1700 but for a different absorber pad.

causes.cause: Secondary waste ink pad near capacity
Heavy borderless printing filling secondary absorber

fixSteps.title: Dismiss Warning
fixSteps.instruction: Press OK or Resume to continue printing.

fixSteps.title: Minimize Waste Ink
fixSteps.instruction: Reduce borderless printing and manual cleaning cycles to slow the fill rate.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Canon support to schedule ink absorber replacement before the printer stops completely.

fixSteps.title: Consider Printer Age
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer is several years old and nearing end of life, consider replacement versus the cost of absorber service.

whenToCallPro: When the absorber becomes full and the printer stops (5B01).

faq.q: Is 1701 different from 1700?
faq.a: Both indicate waste ink absorber warnings. 1701 refers to the secondary absorber pad. The resolution is the same for both.

faq.q: Will my printer stop working?
faq.a: Not immediately. The printer will stop only when the absorber is completely full, indicated by error 5B00 or 5B01.

faq.q: Can I use an external waste ink container?
faq.a: Third-party waste ink kits route ink to an external container, bypassing the internal absorber. This is not officially supported but is a common DIY solution.

title: Carriage Error / Printhead Carriage Stall
shortDescription: The printhead carriage is unable to move or has stalled.
fullDescription: Support Code 5100 indicates the printhead carriage cannot move along its rail. This is a common Canon error caused by paper jams blocking the carriage, a dirty encoder strip, foreign objects in the carriage path, or a failed carriage motor.

causes.cause: Paper or debris blocking carriage movement
Ink cartridge not seated properly lifting against cover

symptoms: Carriage makes grinding or clicking noise

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top cover and look for any paper, packaging material, or foreign objects blocking the carriage path. Remove anything found.

fixSteps.title: Verify Cartridge Seating
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all cartridges are fully pushed down. A cartridge that sits too high can catch on the cover and stall the carriage.

fixSteps.title: Clean Encoder Strip
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thin transparent strip running behind the carriage. Gently clean it with a soft lint-free cloth dampened with water. Do not use alcohol as it can damage the strip.

fixSteps.title: Test Carriage Movement
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer off and unplugged, gently push the carriage left and right to verify it slides freely. If it binds, identify and resolve the source of friction.

whenToCallPro: If the carriage belt is broken or the motor has failed, professional repair is needed.

faq.q: What is the encoder strip?
faq.a: The encoder strip is a thin transparent plastic strip with fine markings that runs horizontally inside the printer. The printer reads these markings to track carriage position. If dirty or damaged, the printer loses track of where the carriage is.

faq.q: Can I push the carriage by hand?
faq.a: Only when the printer is off and unplugged. Never force the carriage while the printer is on, as this can damage the motor and electronics.

faq.q: Why does 5100 keep coming back?
faq.a: A dirty encoder strip is the most common cause of recurring 5100 errors. Thorough cleaning of the encoder strip usually provides a lasting fix.

title: Print Head Overheat / Temperature Error
shortDescription: The print head temperature has exceeded safe operating limits.
fullDescription: Support Code 5200 indicates the print head has overheated or the printer's temperature monitoring has detected an abnormal condition. This can be caused by a failed print head heater circuit, excessive printing without cooldown, or an ink cartridge running completely dry.

causes.cause: Print head temperature sensor detecting overheat
Ink cartridge completely empty causing dry fire

symptoms: Error during or after heavy printing
Print head area hot to touch
Error after running out of ink

fixSteps.title: Power Off and Cool Down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer and let it cool for at least 15 minutes before attempting any troubleshooting.

fixSteps.title: Check and Replace Empty Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Check all cartridge ink levels. Replace any empty or very low cartridges. Printing with empty cartridges causes the print head to overheat.

fixSteps.title: Clean Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: After cooling, run the print head deep cleaning utility from the maintenance menu to clear any clogged nozzles.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If the error recurs after replacing cartridges and cleaning, the print head's thermal management circuit has failed and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the print head thermal circuit has failed and replacement does not resolve the issue.

faq.q: Can running out of ink damage the print head?
faq.a: Yes, printing with an empty cartridge causes the print head to fire without ink, generating excess heat. This is called dry firing and can permanently damage nozzles.

faq.q: How long should I wait for cool-down?
faq.a: Wait at least 15 minutes. In hot environments or after very heavy use, wait up to 30 minutes.

faq.q: Does Canon cover this under warranty?
faq.a: If the printer is under warranty and the cause is not user-induced (like printing with empty cartridges), Canon may cover print head replacement.

title: Print Head Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The print head temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Support Code 5400 indicates a failure of the print head temperature monitoring sensor. The printer cannot verify the print head temperature is within safe operating range, so it stops operation as a safety precaution.

causes.cause: Electrical contact issue between head and printer

symptoms: Printer shuts down as safety precaution
Error appears at startup or during printing

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 2 minutes, then restart. A temporary sensor glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Clean Print Head Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all electrical contacts on the print head and carriage, then reseat the head.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates that may address sensor reading issues.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: The temperature sensor is integrated into the print head. If the sensor has failed, the print head must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists with a new print head, the printer's main board sensor circuit may be faulty.

faq.q: Is this a safety concern?
faq.a: The printer stops as a safety precaution. Without a working temperature sensor, it cannot ensure the print head does not overheat.

faq.q: Can cleaning really fix a sensor error?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the sensor communication is disrupted by dirty contacts, cleaning restores the connection. If the sensor itself has failed, replacement is needed.

faq.q: Is the temperature sensor separate from the print head?
faq.a: No, on Canon inkjet printers, the temperature sensor is integrated into the print head assembly and cannot be replaced separately.

title: Paper Feed Detection Error
shortDescription: The paper feed mechanism detected an error during paper transport.
fullDescription: Support Code 5700 indicates the paper feed system detected an abnormality during paper transport. The paper may have skewed, multi-fed, or the sensor detected unexpected paper behavior that does not match normal feeding patterns.

causes.cause: Feed rollers worn causing inconsistent feed

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reload Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper from the tray, fan it, and reload with even edges. Adjust guides to fit snugly.

fixSteps.title: Clean Feed Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Run the built-in roller cleaning function from the maintenance menu. Repeat 2-3 times.

fixSteps.title: Check Paper Quality
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure paper is not damp, curled, or wrinkled. Try a fresh ream of paper.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 30 seconds, reconnect and restart.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after paper replacement and roller cleaning, the paper sensor may need professional inspection.

faq.q: What causes multi-feeding?
faq.a: Static electricity between sheets, overfilled trays, or worn separation pads. Fanning paper before loading helps reduce static.

faq.q: Can humidity cause this error?
faq.a: Yes, high humidity causes paper to absorb moisture, making sheets stick together and causing multi-feed or skew.

faq.q: Should I use a different paper brand?
faq.a: If multi-feeding persists, try a different paper brand. Paper quality varies and some brands separate more easily in the feed mechanism.

title: Ink Absorber Full
shortDescription: The ink absorber (waste ink pad) is completely full and the printer has stopped.
fullDescription: Support Code 5B00 indicates the primary ink absorber pads are completely full. This is the escalation from the 1700 warning. The printer stops all operation to prevent ink overflow. The absorber must be replaced and the counter reset before the printer can be used again.

causes.cause: Waste ink pads saturated from accumulated cleaning cycles
Extended printer use over months or years
Heavy borderless photo printing producing excess waste ink

symptoms: Power and alarm lights blinking alternately
Cannot print, scan, copy, or fax

fixSteps.title: Acknowledge the Error
fixSteps.instruction: This error requires physical service. The waste ink pads inside the printer must be replaced or cleaned.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Canon support or an authorized service center to schedule ink absorber replacement. Canon may offer mail-in service.

fixSteps.title: DIY Absorber Replacement (Advanced)
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable with disassembly: open the printer, locate the waste ink pads (sponge-like material at the bottom), replace with new pads or clean and wring out existing ones.

fixSteps.title: Reset Waste Counter
fixSteps.instruction: After replacing the absorber, use the Canon Service Tool software to reset the waste ink counter. The printer will not work until the counter is reset.

whenToCallPro: Canon officially recommends professional service for this error. DIY repair is possible but voids the warranty if still active.

faq.q: Can I continue printing?
faq.a: No, the printer is completely disabled by 5B00. All functions are blocked until the absorber is serviced and the counter is reset.

faq.q: How much does Canon charge for this service?
faq.a: Canon service costs typically range from $50 to $100. Some users find it more cost-effective to replace the printer if it is older.

faq.q: Can I just reset the counter without replacing the pads?
faq.a: You can, but this is not recommended. The pads are full and could overflow, causing ink to leak out of the printer and damage surfaces.

title: Secondary Ink Absorber Full
shortDescription: The secondary ink absorber pad is completely full.
fullDescription: Support Code 5B01 indicates the secondary or auxiliary ink absorber is completely full. This functions identically to 5B00 but for a secondary absorber pad. The printer stops all operations until the absorber is serviced.

causes.cause: Secondary waste ink pad fully saturated
Heavy borderless printing on secondary pad
Multiple cleaning cycles accumulating in secondary pad

fixSteps.title: Same as 5B00 Resolution
fixSteps.instruction: The resolution is identical to 5B00. The secondary absorber must be replaced and the counter reset.

fixSteps.title: Contact Service Center
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Canon or an authorized service provider for absorber replacement.

fixSteps.title: DIY Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Disassemble the printer to access the secondary waste ink pad. Replace and reset the counter using the Canon Service Tool.

fixSteps.title: Evaluate Printer Value
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer is older, compare the cost of service against replacing the printer with a newer model.

whenToCallPro: Same guidance as 5B00. Professional service is officially recommended.

faq.q: Is 5B01 different from 5B00?
faq.a: They are functionally the same error for different absorber pads. The resolution and service procedures are identical.

faq.q: Can I get both 5B00 and 5B01?
faq.a: Yes, a heavily used printer can fill both primary and secondary absorbers. Both must be serviced.

faq.q: How do I prevent waste ink absorber issues?
faq.a: Minimize unnecessary printhead cleaning and avoid borderless printing when not needed. These are the primary sources of waste ink.

title: Printer Hardware Error (General)
shortDescription: A general hardware error has been detected in the printer mechanism.
fullDescription: Support Code 6000 is a general hardware error indicating the printer's internal mechanism has encountered an issue it cannot self-resolve. This catch-all code can represent various mechanical failures including motor errors, sensor malfunctions, or mechanical binding.

causes.cause: Internal paper jam not detected by specific jam sensor

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 2 minutes, reconnect and turn on. Many transient hardware errors clear with a full reset.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper and Debris
fixSteps.instruction: Open all access points and check for jammed paper, torn fragments, or foreign objects anywhere in the printer.

fixSteps.title: Verify Carriage Movement
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer off, check that the carriage moves freely. Also check that all covers, trays, and units are properly seated.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Support
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after basic troubleshooting, contact Canon support as the error likely indicates an internal mechanical failure.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after clearing all visible issues and power cycling, internal hardware diagnosis is needed.

faq.q: Is 6000 a serious error?
faq.a: It can be. If it clears with a power cycle, it was likely a transient issue. If it persists, it indicates a mechanical failure requiring service.

faq.q: What kind of hardware failure does 6000 indicate?
faq.a: The 6000 code is general and does not specify which component failed. A technician needs to diagnose the specific cause.

faq.q: Should I try to fix this myself?
faq.a: Basic steps like clearing jams and power cycling are safe to try. Do not attempt to disassemble the internal mechanism without experience.

title: Paper Feed Cam Sensor Error
shortDescription: The paper feed cam sensor has detected an abnormal position.
fullDescription: Support Code 6A00 indicates the paper feed cam mechanism is not in its expected position. The cam controls paper feeding timing and position. If the cam sensor detects the mechanism is out of position, the printer stops to prevent damage.

causes.cause: Paper jam forcing cam mechanism out of position
Cam mechanism gear stripped or broken
Foreign object in paper path affecting cam

fixSteps.title: Clear Paper Path
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper and check thoroughly for any jammed paper or foreign objects in the paper path.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off, unplug for 2 minutes, reconnect and restart. The mechanism may reset to the correct position.

fixSteps.title: Multiple Power Cycles
fixSteps.instruction: If one power cycle does not clear the error, try 3-5 power cycles. Each cycle attempts to reset the cam position.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the cam mechanism is physically damaged, it requires professional repair to replace the cam assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the cam mechanism is physically damaged (stripped gears, broken sensor), professional repair is required.

faq.q: What is the paper feed cam?
faq.a: The cam is a mechanical component that controls the timing and position of the paper feed rollers. It ensures paper is grabbed and fed at the correct time.

faq.q: Can multiple power cycles really fix this?
faq.a: Yes, sometimes the cam is just out of position and the printer can reset it during initialization. Multiple attempts give it the best chance of self-correcting.

faq.q: Is 6A00 expensive to repair?
faq.a: If the cam mechanism is physically broken, repair costs can approach the price of a new consumer printer. For inexpensive models, replacement may be more economical.

title: Paper Feed Position Error
shortDescription: The paper feed mechanism detected a position error.
fullDescription: Support Code 6A80 indicates the paper feed mechanism's position encoder detected an error. The printer cannot confirm the paper feed is in the correct position to operate, typically due to a mechanical binding or cam position issue.

causes.cause: Motor unable to drive feed mechanism

symptoms: Paper feed mechanism not moving correctly

fixSteps.title: Thorough Paper Path Check
fixSteps.instruction: Check every accessible area of the paper path for fragments, debris, or foreign objects. Use a flashlight and inspect carefully.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle Multiple Times
fixSteps.instruction: Perform 3-5 power cycles with the printer unplugged for 2 minutes each time.

fixSteps.title: Clean Internal Areas
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow dust and debris from the internal paper path and feed mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Professional Repair
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the feed mechanism requires professional diagnosis and repair.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after thorough cleaning and multiple power cycles.

faq.q: Is 6A80 the same as 6A00?
faq.a: They are related but different. Both involve the paper feed mechanism. 6A80 specifically relates to position sensing while 6A00 relates to the cam sensor.

faq.q: Can I open the printer to fix this?
faq.a: Basic cleaning is safe, but the feed mechanism is deep inside the printer. Attempting to access it without experience can cause additional damage.

faq.q: How common is this error?
faq.a: 6A80 is moderately common, especially in printers that have experienced paper jams. The jam clearance process can sometimes displace the feed mechanism.

title: Paper Feed Mechanism Failure
shortDescription: The paper feed mechanism has failed to operate correctly.
fullDescription: Support Code 6A81 indicates a more severe paper feed mechanism failure than 6A80. The mechanism has not only misread its position but has failed to operate within normal parameters, suggesting a mechanical failure rather than a temporary misalignment.

causes.cause: Feed mechanism gear broken or stripped

symptoms: No paper feed movement at all
Loud clicking or grinding from feed area
Error does not clear after multiple power cycles

fixSteps.title: Clear All Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper and thoroughly inspect for jams. Check rear transport unit.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect, and restart. Listen for normal motor sounds during initialization.

fixSteps.title: Clean Feed Area
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to clean the paper feed area of dust and debris that may be binding the mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service Required
fixSteps.instruction: 6A81 usually indicates a mechanical failure. Contact Canon service for repair.

whenToCallPro: 6A81 almost always requires professional service due to the mechanical nature of the failure.

faq.q: Is 6A81 repairable?
faq.a: Yes, but it requires professional service. The feed mechanism gears or motor may need replacement.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing?
faq.a: For consumer PIXMA printers, compare the repair cost against a new printer. For higher-end models, repair is usually worthwhile.

faq.q: What caused the feed mechanism to fail?
faq.a: Most commonly, severe paper jams that forced the mechanism beyond its normal range, or simply wear from high usage over time.

title: Purge Unit Error
shortDescription: The printhead purge/cleaning unit has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Support Code 6B00 indicates the purge unit (ink cleaning station) has failed. The purge unit is responsible for capping the printhead when not in use and performing cleaning cycles. A failure prevents proper printhead maintenance.

causes.cause: Purge unit mechanism jammed with dried ink
Electrical connection to purge unit lost

symptoms: Cleaning cycles fail to start or complete

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off, unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Clear Carriage Area
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cover and ensure the carriage can move to the home position (usually right side). Clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Clean Purge Unit Area
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, clean the rubber caps and surrounding area of the purge unit (located where the carriage parks) with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: The purge unit is a sealed assembly that typically requires professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: Most 6B00 errors require professional service as the purge unit is an internal sealed component.

faq.q: What is the purge unit?
faq.a: The purge unit is the cleaning station where the printhead parks. It includes rubber caps that seal the nozzles, a wiper blade, and a suction pump for cleaning cycles.

faq.q: Can dried ink cause this?
faq.a: Yes, dried ink can jam the purge unit mechanism. This is common in printers that sit unused for extended periods.

faq.q: How do I prevent purge unit failure?
faq.a: Print at least once a week to keep the printhead and purge unit active. Prolonged inactivity allows ink to dry in the mechanism.

title: Waste Ink Transport Error
shortDescription: The waste ink transport mechanism has failed.
fullDescription: Support Code 6C10 indicates a failure in the waste ink transport system, which moves waste ink from the purge unit to the ink absorber pads. A blockage or pump failure prevents proper waste ink management.

causes.cause: Waste ink tube blocked or kinked

symptoms: Ink visible in unusual locations inside printer

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off, unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Visible Issues
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer and look for any visible ink leaks, kinked tubes, or displaced components.

fixSteps.title: Clean Purge Area
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, clean the purge unit and surrounding area of dried ink buildup.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: Internal waste ink transport issues typically require professional disassembly and repair.

whenToCallPro: Most 6C10 errors require professional service.

faq.q: Is this related to the ink absorber errors?
faq.a: Yes, the waste ink transport system feeds into the ink absorber pads. A transport failure can prevent proper waste ink management even if the absorber is not full.

faq.q: Can I see the waste ink tubes?
faq.a: Some tubes may be visible inside the printer, but most are routed internally and require disassembly to access.

faq.q: Is this a common error?
faq.a: 6C10 is less common than other errors. It typically occurs in heavily used printers or those that have been inactive for long periods.

title: Print Head Voltage / Critical Hardware Error
shortDescription: A critical print head or hardware error has occurred requiring immediate attention.
fullDescription: Support Code B200 is one of the most serious Canon printer errors. It indicates the print head is receiving abnormal voltage, has a severe malfunction, or the printer's main board has detected a critical hardware failure. This error completely disables the printer.

causes.cause: Print head electrical failure or short circuit
Power supply issue providing incorrect voltage
Ink cartridge causing electrical issue with print head

symptoms: Error appears at startup before printing

fixSteps.title: Power Reset with Head Removal
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 5 minutes. Open the cover, remove all cartridges and the print head. Wait 10 minutes. Reinstall the print head (without cartridges), close cover, plug in and turn on. If it powers up without B200, install cartridges one at a time.

fixSteps.title: Clean Print Head Thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the print head and soak the nozzle plate in warm distilled water for 15 minutes. Clean all contacts with isopropyl alcohol. Dry thoroughly before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Test with Different Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Try a complete set of new or known-good cartridges. A defective cartridge can cause the print head to report a voltage error.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the print head has likely failed. Install a new print head.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after replacing both the print head and all cartridges, the main board is likely faulty. Professional service or printer replacement is needed.

faq.q: Is B200 a death sentence for my printer?
faq.a: Not always. While B200 is serious, many cases are resolved by replacing the print head or thorough cleaning. Only main board failures are typically uneconomical to repair.

faq.q: Why does the power reset with head removal sometimes work?
faq.a: Removing the head and cartridges allows the printer to initialize without the load of the print head. This can reset the voltage monitoring circuit.

faq.q: Can a single bad cartridge cause B200?
faq.a: Yes, a cartridge with a defective chip or short circuit can cause the print head to report abnormal voltage. Testing with known-good cartridges is an important diagnostic step.

title: Print Head or Board Hardware Error
shortDescription: A critical hardware error detected in the print head or main board.
fullDescription: Support Code B203 is a critical error similar to B200 but often indicates a more specific failure in the print head logic or the printer's main control board. The printer stops all operations.

causes.cause: Print head connector corrosion or damage

symptoms: No response to any controls except power off

fixSteps.title: Full Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 10 minutes. Remove the print head and all cartridges. Reconnect and power on.

fixSteps.title: Clean All Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly clean all electrical contacts on the print head, cartridges, and inside the carriage.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: Install a new print head to rule out print head failure.

fixSteps.title: Professional Assessment
fixSteps.instruction: If a new print head does not resolve B203, the main board is likely faulty. Assess repair viability.

whenToCallPro: If print head replacement does not clear the error.

faq.q: Is B203 worse than B200?
faq.a: They are similarly severe. B203 may be slightly more likely to indicate a main board issue rather than just a print head problem.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause B203?
faq.a: Yes, electrical surges can damage the print head electronics or main board, triggering B203. Use a surge protector.

faq.q: Should I try a new print head before giving up?
faq.a: Yes, always try a new print head first. It is the most common fix and much cheaper than main board replacement.

title: Print Head Communication Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot communicate with the print head.
fullDescription: Support Code B204 indicates a total communication failure between the printer and the print head. Unlike B200/B203 which may involve voltage issues, B204 specifically indicates the data communication channel has failed.
symptoms: Error persists after print head cleaning

fixSteps.title: Clean Data Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the print head and meticulously clean the data contacts (the fine gold or copper pins) on both the head and carriage with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Carriage Cable
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the flex cable (flat ribbon cable) that connects the carriage to the printer body. Check for kinks, tears, or visible damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: Try a new print head to determine if the head's data circuit has failed.

fixSteps.title: Professional Repair
fixSteps.instruction: If a new print head does not resolve the error, the carriage flex cable or main board needs professional service.

whenToCallPro: If a new print head still shows B204, the carriage cable or board needs professional replacement.

faq.q: What is the carriage flex cable?
faq.a: The flex cable is a flat ribbon cable that carries data and power signals from the main board to the moving carriage. It flexes with each carriage movement and can wear over time.

faq.q: Can a damaged flex cable be repaired?
faq.a: Flex cables are typically replaced rather than repaired. They are inexpensive parts but require significant disassembly to access.

faq.q: How do I prevent B204?
faq.a: Avoid pulling or touching the flex cable. Do not force the carriage. Keep the printer clean to prevent ink contamination on contacts.

title: General Printer Error
shortDescription: An unspecified hardware error has been detected.
fullDescription: Support Code C000 is a general error indicating the printer detected a hardware issue that does not correspond to a more specific error code. It can represent various internal failures.
symptoms: Error may be intermittent or persistent

fixSteps.title: Full Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 5 minutes, then reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Remove All Consumables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove cartridges, print head, and all paper. Check for any visible issues. Reinstall everything.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer partially functions, try updating firmware through Canon's IJ Utility.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Support
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, contact Canon support for diagnosis.

whenToCallPro: If the error does not clear after basic troubleshooting, professional diagnosis is needed.

faq.q: Is C000 a common error?
faq.a: C000 is relatively uncommon. It typically indicates an issue that does not match more specific error patterns.

faq.q: Can firmware cause C000?
faq.a: In rare cases, firmware bugs can trigger C000. Updating to the latest firmware is always a good troubleshooting step.

faq.q: Is C000 repairable?
faq.a: It depends on the underlying cause. Some C000 errors resolve with power cycling; others indicate hardware failures that may be costly to repair.

title: Paper Jam (P-Series Code)
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer.
fullDescription: Error P02 is the P-series paper jam code. On Canon printers that use the P-error system instead of support codes, P02 indicates paper stuck inside the printer that needs to be cleared.

causes.cause: Paper jammed in feed or transport area
Wrong paper type causing feed issue

fixSteps.title: Turn Off Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Power off the printer before attempting to remove jammed paper.

fixSteps.title: Open Rear Cover
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover or transport unit. Carefully pull jammed paper straight out.

fixSteps.title: Check All Areas
fixSteps.instruction: Check the output tray, cartridge area, and feed area for any remaining paper pieces.

fixSteps.title: Clean Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Run the roller cleaning function or manually clean feed rollers with a damp cloth.

whenToCallPro: If jams recur after clearing and cleaning, the feed mechanism may need service.

faq.q: Is P02 the same as Support Code 1300?
faq.a: They indicate the same issue (paper jam) but use different error code formats. P02 is used on older or entry-level Canon models.

faq.q: Why does P02 keep appearing?
faq.a: Recurring P02 errors usually indicate dirty feed rollers or paper that does not meet specifications. Clean rollers and use fresh paper.

faq.q: Can I prevent paper jams?
faq.a: Fan paper before loading, do not overfill the tray, use paper within specifications, and keep the printer clean.

title: Paper Jam in Feed / Carriage Position Error
shortDescription: Paper jam detected or carriage position cannot be determined.
fullDescription: Error P03 can indicate either a paper jam that the printer cannot clear, or a carriage positioning error. The printer's internal sensors detect an abnormal condition in the paper path or carriage area.

causes.cause: Encoder strip dirty preventing position tracking

fixSteps.title: Clear Paper Jam
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cover and carefully remove any paper or debris in the carriage path and paper path.

fixSteps.title: Check Carriage Movement
fixSteps.instruction: With printer off and unplugged, gently push the carriage left and right to verify free movement.

fixSteps.title: Clean Encoder Strip
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the transparent encoder strip running behind the carriage with a soft damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes, reconnect and restart.

whenToCallPro: If the carriage belt or motor has failed.

faq.q: Is P03 a paper jam or carriage error?
faq.a: It can be either. Start by clearing any paper jams, then check carriage movement. The most common cause is paper debris blocking the carriage.

faq.q: What is the encoder strip?
faq.a: A transparent plastic strip with fine markings used to track the printhead carriage position. Dirt or damage causes positioning errors.

faq.q: Can I replace the encoder strip?
faq.a: Encoder strips are available as replacement parts but require careful installation. Cleaning is usually sufficient unless the strip is physically damaged.

title: Ink Absorber Full (P-Series)
shortDescription: The ink absorber is full and the printer has stopped.
fullDescription: Error P07 is the P-series equivalent of Support Code 5B00. The waste ink absorber pads are completely full and the printer has stopped all operations. The absorber must be serviced and the counter reset.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: Canon recommends contacting an authorized service center for ink absorber replacement.

fixSteps.title: DIY Absorber Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: For experienced users: disassemble the printer bottom, remove the saturated pads, replace with new absorber material.

fixSteps.title: Reset Counter
fixSteps.instruction: After pad replacement, use the Canon Service Tool to reset the waste ink counter.

fixSteps.title: Evaluate Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Consider whether the cost of service exceeds the value of the printer. Older models may be more economical to replace.

whenToCallPro: Canon officially recommends professional service for all ink absorber issues.

faq.q: Is P07 the same as 5B00?
faq.a: Yes, they indicate the same issue. P07 is used on printers with the P-error code system, while 5B00 uses the support code system.

faq.q: How do I get the Canon Service Tool?
faq.a: The Canon Service Tool is a utility used by service centers. It is available online from third-party sources but is not officially distributed to consumers.

faq.q: Can I use the printer for scanning only?
faq.a: Unfortunately, P07 disables all printer functions including scanning and copying, even though those do not use the waste ink system.

title: Ink Absorber Almost Full (P-Series Warning)
shortDescription: The ink absorber is nearly full.
fullDescription: Error P08 is the P-series warning that the waste ink absorber is approaching capacity. This is the P-series equivalent of Support Code 1700. Printing can continue but the printer will eventually stop with a P07 error.

causes.cause: Normal waste ink accumulation approaching capacity

symptoms: Can be dismissed to continue printing

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Resume/Stop button to dismiss the warning and continue printing.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Waste Ink Generation
fixSteps.instruction: Minimize manual cleaning cycles and unnecessary borderless printing.

fixSteps.title: Plan for Service
fixSteps.instruction: Begin planning for absorber service or printer replacement.

fixSteps.title: Schedule Service
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Canon service before the absorber is completely full to avoid unexpected printer downtime.

whenToCallPro: Plan to service before the absorber reaches full capacity (P07).

faq.q: How long can I print after P08?
faq.a: Weeks to months depending on usage. Minimize cleaning cycles to extend the time before the absorber fills completely.

faq.q: Can I reset P08 without servicing?
faq.a: The warning resets after the absorber is serviced. You cannot reset it independently. Pressing Resume simply dismisses the current warning.

faq.q: Is P08 urgent?
faq.a: Not immediately. It is a planning alert. You have time to arrange service before the printer stops with P07.

title: Printer Hardware Error (P-Series)
shortDescription: A hardware error has been detected that requires service.
fullDescription: Error P10 is a general hardware error in the P-series code system. It indicates an internal mechanical or electronic fault that the printer cannot self-resolve. This is similar to Support Code 6000.

causes.cause: Paper or debris causing internal obstruction

symptoms: Error at startup or during printing

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all access points and check for paper, debris, or displaced components.

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reseat Components
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reseat the print head, cartridges, and paper trays.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, professional service is needed.

whenToCallPro: If basic troubleshooting does not clear the error.

faq.q: What specific hardware does P10 indicate?
faq.a: P10 is a general code that does not specify which component failed. A technician needs to diagnose the specific cause.

faq.q: Can P10 be caused by a power outage?
faq.a: Yes, power fluctuations can trigger P10. A power cycle usually resolves transient electrical issues.

faq.q: Is P10 repairable?
faq.a: It depends on the underlying cause. Some P10 errors clear with simple troubleshooting; others require professional repair.

title: No Paper (E-Series Code)
shortDescription: No paper detected in the printer.
fullDescription: Error E02 is the E-series code for no paper detected. On Canon printers using the E-code system (typically entry-level models), E02 means the paper tray is empty or the paper sensor cannot detect loaded paper.

causes.cause: Paper not pushed in far enough
Paper guides not adjusted to paper

fixSteps.title: Load Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Load paper in the rear tray or cassette and adjust the guides.

fixSteps.title: Push Paper to Back Edge
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the paper is pushed against the rear edge of the tray and the guides are snug.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: After loading paper, press the Resume/Stop button to clear the error and retry.

fixSteps.title: Clean Sensor Area
fixSteps.instruction: If paper is loaded but not detected, use compressed air to clean the paper sensor area.

whenToCallPro: Only if the paper sensor has physically failed.

faq.q: Which paper tray should I use?
faq.a: Load paper in the tray indicated by your print settings. Most E-series printers have a rear tray for general paper and may have a cassette for plain paper.

faq.q: Why does E02 appear when paper is loaded?
faq.a: The paper may not be pushed far enough in, or the guides are not touching the paper edges. Adjust both to ensure proper detection.

faq.q: Can I use thick paper?
faq.a: Check your printer specifications. Most E-series printers support up to 105 g/m2 from the rear tray. Heavier paper may not be detected correctly.

title: Paper Jam (E-Series Code)
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer.
fullDescription: Error E03 is the E-series paper jam code. Paper is stuck inside the printer and must be cleared before printing can resume.

causes.cause: Paper jam in feed or print area

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover and gently pull jammed paper straight out in the direction of travel.

fixSteps.title: Check Inside Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top cover and check the carriage area for torn paper pieces.

fixSteps.title: Reload Paper Properly
fixSteps.instruction: Fan and reload paper, ensuring guides are properly adjusted.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Resume button to clear the error and attempt to print again.

whenToCallPro: If jams recur constantly despite proper paper loading and roller cleaning.

faq.q: Is E03 the same as Support Code 1300?
faq.a: Yes, they indicate the same paper jam condition. E03 is used on E-series models.

faq.q: What if I cannot see any jammed paper?
faq.a: Small paper fragments may be stuck inside. Check with a flashlight through all access openings. Sometimes a tiny piece causes persistent E03 errors.

faq.q: Can I prevent E03 errors?
faq.a: Use good quality paper, do not overfill the tray, fan paper before loading, and clean rollers regularly.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Installed Correctly (E-Series)
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges are not properly installed.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the printer cannot detect one or more ink cartridges. The cartridge may be missing, improperly installed, or have a faulty chip.

causes.cause: Cartridge not fully clicked into slot
Protective tape not removed from cartridge

fixSteps.title: Reseat Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cover, remove all cartridges, and reinsert each one firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Remove Protective Tape
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the orange protective tape has been removed from each cartridge. Leave the electrical contact area exposed.

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the cartridge chip contacts with a lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Try Different Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If one specific cartridge causes the error, try a replacement.

whenToCallPro: Not typically needed for cartridge issues.

faq.q: Do I need to remove all tape from the cartridge?
faq.a: Remove the orange protective tab and any visible tape. Do not remove or touch the copper contact chip.

faq.q: Can I use generic cartridges?
faq.a: Third-party cartridges may work but can trigger recognition errors. Canon-original cartridges are most reliable.

faq.q: Which cartridges does my E-series printer use?
faq.a: Check the label inside the cartridge access door or your printer's user manual for the correct cartridge numbers.

title: Print Head Error (E-Series)
shortDescription: The print head cannot be detected or has a problem.
fullDescription: Error E05 on E-series Canon printers indicates a print head issue. The print head may not be properly installed, may have dirty contacts, or may have failed.

causes.cause: Print head contacts dirty or corroded
Ink cartridge causing print head error

fixSteps.title: Reseat Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: Remove cartridges, lift the print head lock lever, remove the print head, wait 10 seconds, and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Clean Print Head Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Clean electrical contacts on the print head and carriage with a lint-free cloth dampened with distilled water.

fixSteps.title: Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 2 minutes while the print head is removed. Reinstall the head, reconnect, and power on.

fixSteps.title: Replace Print Head
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning and reseating do not resolve the error, the print head needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists with a new print head, the carriage electronics may need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: Is E05 the same as Support Code 1401?
faq.a: They indicate similar print head issues but in different code formats. E05 is used on E-series printers.

faq.q: Can I use the printer for scanning with E05?
faq.a: On some models, scanning may still work even with a print head error. Try scanning from the printer control panel.

faq.q: How much does a Canon print head cost?
faq.a: Canon print heads typically cost $25-$60 depending on the model. Check Canon's parts store or authorized dealers for your specific model.

title: Ink Level Low (E-Series)
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges are running low.
fullDescription: Error E13 indicates low ink levels in one or more cartridges. This is a warning rather than a critical error, and the printer can continue printing. Quality may gradually degrade as ink runs out.

causes.cause: Heavy photo or graphics printing depleting ink faster

fixSteps.title: Continue Printing
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Resume button to continue printing. Quality may degrade gradually.

fixSteps.title: Check Ink Levels
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Canon IJ printer utility or check the printer display to see which cartridge is low.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacements
fixSteps.instruction: Order replacement cartridges for the low colors so they are ready when needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace When Quality Degrades
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the cartridge when print quality becomes unacceptable.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. This is a consumable replacement.

faq.q: How accurate are Canon ink level estimates?
faq.a: Canon ink level indicators are estimates based on usage patterns, not direct measurement. Actual remaining ink may vary.

faq.q: Can I disable the low ink warning?
faq.a: On some models, you can disable ink level monitoring. However, this means the printer will not warn you when ink is critically low, risking damage from dry printing.

faq.q: Should I replace immediately?
faq.a: No, continue printing until quality degrades. E13 is an early warning that gives you time to order replacements.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Recognized (E-Series)
shortDescription: The printer cannot identify the installed ink cartridge.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates the printer cannot communicate with or recognize an installed ink cartridge. The cartridge chip may be dirty, the cartridge may be incompatible, or there is a communication failure.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and firmly reinstall the unrecognized cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the chip on the cartridge with a lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Verify Compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the cartridge number matches your printer's requirements.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If the cartridge is confirmed compatible and contacts are clean, try a different cartridge as the chip may be defective.

whenToCallPro: Not usually needed for cartridge recognition issues.

faq.q: Can refilled cartridges cause E14?
faq.a: Yes, refilled cartridges sometimes have chip issues that prevent recognition. Try a new cartridge to diagnose.

faq.q: Is the cartridge chip reusable?
faq.a: Canon cartridge chips track ink usage. Some third-party resetting tools can reset the chip, but this is not officially supported.

faq.q: What if multiple cartridges show E14?
faq.a: If all cartridges trigger E14, the issue is likely with the print head contacts or the printer's cartridge reader, not the cartridges themselves.

title: Ink Cartridge Empty (E-Series)
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges are empty.
fullDescription: Error E16 indicates one or more ink cartridges have been estimated as empty. The printer stops to prevent print head damage from dry firing. Replacement cartridges are required to continue printing.

causes.cause: Ink fully consumed through normal printing
Cartridge installed with very low ink

symptoms: Pressing Resume may allow limited black-only printing

fixSteps.title: Identify Empty Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Check the display or printer utility to see which cartridge needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the empty cartridge and install a new one. Remove protective tape from the new cartridge before insertion.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume for Temporary Use
fixSteps.instruction: If you do not have a replacement, press and hold the Resume button for 5+ seconds. The printer may allow limited printing with remaining ink at your own risk.

fixSteps.title: Print Test Page
fixSteps.instruction: After replacement, print a test page to verify proper operation.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Cartridge replacement is a user task.

faq.q: Can I override E16 and keep printing?
faq.a: On some Canon models, pressing and holding the Resume button for 5+ seconds disables ink level monitoring temporarily. Canon warns this can damage the print head.

faq.q: Why does one color run out faster?
faq.a: Black typically runs out first due to higher usage in text printing. Color cartridges may also deplete unevenly depending on the color content of your prints.

faq.q: Can I use the printer in black-only mode?
faq.a: Some Canon models allow black-only printing when color cartridges are empty, but this varies by model. Check your printer's documentation.

title: Scanner Unit Error (E-Series)
shortDescription: The scanner unit has encountered an error.
fullDescription: Error E59 indicates a failure of the scanner unit on Canon MFP printers using the E-code system. The scanner mechanism cannot initialize or operate correctly.
symptoms: Scanner does not move during scan attempt

fixSteps.title: Check Scanner Glass
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanner lid and check for objects on the glass or in the scanner path.

fixSteps.title: Check Scanner Lock
fixSteps.instruction: If your model has a scanner transport lock, ensure it is in the unlocked position.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the scanner mechanism has failed, professional service is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the scanner motor or belt has failed mechanically.

faq.q: Can I still print with E59?
faq.a: On most Canon MFP printers, the print function operates independently of the scanner. You should still be able to print from your computer.

faq.q: Does E59 affect all scanner functions?
faq.a: Yes, E59 disables scanning, copying, and fax (on models with fax) as these all require the scanner mechanism.

faq.q: Is scanner repair worth the cost?
faq.a: For entry-level E-series printers, scanner repair often exceeds the cost of a new printer. Consider replacement for older models.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Installed
shortDescription: An ink cartridge is not installed in the required slot.
fullDescription: Support Code 1660 indicates one or more ink cartridge slots are empty. All cartridge slots must be populated for the printer to operate, even if you only print in black and white.

causes.cause: Cartridge removed for inspection and not replaced
Cartridge not seated deep enough to register

symptoms: Cartridge indicator light not illuminated for missing slot

fixSteps.title: Install Missing Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge access area and install the missing cartridge in the indicated slot.

fixSteps.title: Press Firmly Until Click
fixSteps.instruction: Push the cartridge down until you hear and feel a click indicating it is locked in place.

fixSteps.title: Remove Protective Materials
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all protective tape and caps have been removed from the new cartridge before installation.

fixSteps.title: Close Cover
fixSteps.instruction: Close the top cover and wait for the printer to recognize the cartridge.

whenToCallPro: Not needed.

faq.q: Do I need all cartridges installed to print?
faq.a: Yes, Canon printers require all cartridge slots to be populated. Even if you only print in black, color cartridges must be installed.

faq.q: Can I use an empty cartridge as a placeholder?
faq.a: An empty cartridge will still be recognized. However, the printer may show low ink warnings and eventually stop if it believes the cartridge is too empty.

faq.q: Why does Canon require all cartridges?
faq.a: Canon printers perform maintenance cleaning on all cartridges during power-on cycles. Missing cartridges can cause air ingestion into the print head, leading to print quality issues.

title: Ink Level Cannot Be Detected
shortDescription: The printer cannot determine the ink level of an installed cartridge.
fullDescription: Support Code 1688 indicates the printer cannot determine how much ink remains in one or more cartridges. This commonly occurs with refilled cartridges, cartridges that have had their ink level monitoring disabled, or after pressing Resume to override a previous empty cartridge warning.

causes.cause: Cartridge ink level monitoring was previously disabled by user
Cartridge from different region or market

symptoms: Ink level shown as unknown or question mark
Printer may still print but without ink level monitoring
Warning about potential print head damage

fixSteps.title: Acknowledge the Warning
fixSteps.instruction: Press Resume to acknowledge that you understand ink levels are not being monitored. The printer will continue printing.

fixSteps.title: Monitor Print Quality Manually
fixSteps.instruction: Without ink level monitoring, watch for fading or streaking in prints. Replace cartridges when quality degrades.

fixSteps.title: Install New OEM Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If you want to restore ink level monitoring, install a new Canon-original cartridge. This resets the monitoring function.

fixSteps.title: Replace Refilled Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If using a refilled cartridge, consider switching to an OEM or compatible cartridge with a functioning ink level chip.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. This is an informational warning.

faq.q: Is it safe to print without ink level monitoring?
faq.a: You can print, but you must manually monitor print quality. Printing with a completely empty cartridge can damage the print head.

faq.q: Will this error go away on its own?
faq.a: No, once ink level monitoring is disabled for a cartridge, it stays disabled until a new cartridge with a fresh chip is installed.

faq.q: Does refilling cartridges void my warranty?
faq.a: Using non-Canon cartridges or refilled cartridges may void the warranty if they cause damage to the printer. Check Canon's warranty terms.

title: Critical System Error
shortDescription: A critical system error requiring service has occurred.
fullDescription: Support Code 9000 is a critical system-level error indicating a major hardware or firmware failure. The printer cannot recover from this state through normal troubleshooting and typically requires professional service or replacement.

fixSteps.title: Full Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 10 minutes. Reconnect and try powering on.

fixSteps.title: Remove All Consumables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove cartridges, print head, and paper. Try powering on with minimal components.

fixSteps.title: Try Different Power Source
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the printer into a different wall outlet to rule out power issues.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: Error 9000 almost always requires professional diagnosis and repair. Contact Canon support.

whenToCallPro: Immediately. Error 9000 indicates a critical failure that cannot be resolved through user troubleshooting.

faq.q: Is 9000 the worst Canon error?
faq.a: It is among the most serious. 9000 indicates a critical system failure that usually requires board replacement or is economically unviable to repair.

faq.q: Should I repair or replace?
faq.a: For consumer PIXMA printers, the cost of main board replacement often approaches or exceeds the cost of a new printer. Enterprise and high-end models may be worth repairing.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause 9000?
faq.a: Yes, significant power surges can damage the main board and trigger 9000. Always use surge protection for electronic equipment.

title: Paper Size Setting Mismatch
shortDescription: The paper size in the print settings does not match the loaded paper.
fullDescription: Support Code 2100 indicates the paper size selected in the print driver does not match the paper loaded in the printer. The printer's sensors detected a different paper width than expected.

causes.cause: Print driver paper size different from loaded paper
Paper guides not adjusted to actual paper size
Wrong tray selected in print settings
Application page setup different from driver settings

symptoms: Printer prompts to verify paper size

fixSteps.title: Check Paper Size
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the actual paper size loaded in the tray. Common sizes are Letter, A4, Legal, and 4x6 photo.

fixSteps.title: Match Driver Settings
fixSteps.instruction: In the print dialog, click Properties/Preferences and set the paper size to match what is loaded.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Paper Guides
fixSteps.instruction: Open the tray and slide the guides to match the loaded paper exactly.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press Resume to retry with the corrected settings.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. This is a settings issue.

faq.q: Why does this happen when I print from my phone?
faq.a: Mobile print apps may default to a paper size different from what is loaded. Check the paper size setting in the mobile app's print options.

faq.q: Can I turn off paper size checking?
faq.a: On some Canon models, you can disable paper size detection in the printer settings. This allows printing regardless of paper size mismatch but may result in clipped content.

faq.q: What is the difference between Letter and A4?
faq.a: Letter is 8.5x11 inches (US standard). A4 is 210x297mm (international standard). A4 is slightly narrower and taller.

title: Paper Type or Quality Error
shortDescription: The printer detected the loaded paper does not match the selected type or quality.
fullDescription: Support Code 2900 indicates the paper type or quality setting in the print driver does not match what the printer detects in the tray. This is important because the printer adjusts ink application and drying time based on the media type.

causes.cause: Paper type in driver set to Photo but plain paper loaded
Media type not set correctly for specialty paper
Paper sensor detecting different surface than expected

symptoms: Printer prompts to check media type
Print quality may be incorrect for the media

fixSteps.title: Verify Paper Type
fixSteps.instruction: Check what type of paper is actually loaded (plain, photo glossy, matte, envelope, etc.).

fixSteps.title: Set Correct Media Type
fixSteps.instruction: In the print driver, set the Media Type to match what is physically loaded.

fixSteps.title: Set Paper Type on Printer
fixSteps.instruction: If prompted on the printer display, confirm or change the paper type to match what is loaded.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: After correcting the settings, press Resume to continue.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. This is a settings issue.

faq.q: Why does media type matter?
faq.a: The printer applies different amounts of ink and uses different drying times based on media type. Photo paper gets more ink than plain paper. Wrong settings can cause smearing or poor quality.

faq.q: What media type should I select for generic photo paper?
faq.a: Select 'Photo Paper Plus Glossy II' for glossy paper or 'Matte Photo Paper' for matte finish. These are the closest matches for most third-party photo papers.

faq.q: Can I disable media type checking?
faq.a: Some Canon models allow disabling this check in the printer's settings menu. However, incorrect media type settings will affect print quality.

title: Paper Output Unit Error
shortDescription: The paper output mechanism has encountered an error.
