fullDescription: Support Code 6D00 indicates a failure in the paper output mechanism. The printer cannot properly eject printed pages into the output tray due to a motor, roller, or sensor failure in the output area.

fixSteps.title: Clear Output Area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any paper from the output tray and check for jammed or stuck pages in the output slot.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Clean Output Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, clean the output rollers with a damp lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the output motor has failed, professional service is required.

whenToCallPro: If the output motor is confirmed non-functional after clearing all obstructions.

faq.q: Is 6D00 common?
faq.a: 6D00 is relatively uncommon. It usually appears after a paper jam in the output area has displaced the output mechanism.

faq.q: Can I still print if the output is broken?
faq.a: No, the printer requires the output mechanism to function in order to properly eject pages and track paper position.

faq.q: Is repair economical for 6D00?
faq.a: The output mechanism is relatively simple. If only the motor needs replacement, repair is usually economical. If the entire output assembly is damaged, compare against printer replacement cost.

title: Internal Electronic Error
shortDescription: An internal electronic error has been detected.
fullDescription: Support Code 6E00 indicates an internal electronic failure. This general code covers various electronic component failures within the printer's control system.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect and restart. Transient electrical issues may clear.

fixSteps.title: Try Different Outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Plug directly into a different wall outlet (not power strip) to rule out power issues.

fixSteps.title: Remove All Accessories
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect USB cables, memory cards, and any external devices. Try powering on with only the power cord.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, an internal electronic component has failed and requires professional diagnosis.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and trying a different outlet.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause 6E00?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage internal electronics. Using a surge protector can prevent this.

faq.q: Is 6E00 repairable?
faq.a: It depends on which electronic component failed. Some failures are fixable; others require board replacement.

faq.q: How common is 6E00?
faq.a: 6E00 is uncommon and usually indicates a genuine hardware failure rather than a user-correctable issue.

title: Printer Mechanism Error
shortDescription: A general printer mechanism error has been detected.
fullDescription: Support Code 6F00 is a general mechanism error indicating the printer's internal moving parts have encountered an issue. This can range from minor obstructions to major mechanical failures.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all access points and check for paper, debris, or foreign objects in the mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check Carriage and Paper Path
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the carriage moves freely and the paper path is clear.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, an internal mechanical component has failed.

whenToCallPro: If basic obstruction clearing and power cycling do not resolve the error.

faq.q: Is 6F00 similar to 6000?
faq.a: Both indicate hardware issues, but 6F00 is more specifically related to mechanical mechanisms while 6000 is a broader general error.

faq.q: Can I hear what is wrong?
faq.a: Often yes. Listen during startup for grinding, clicking, or struggling motor sounds. These can help a technician diagnose the specific failed component.

faq.q: Should I keep trying to power on?
faq.a: Try 2-3 power cycles. If the error persists with abnormal sounds, stop trying to avoid further mechanical damage.

title: No Paper in Cassette
shortDescription: The paper cassette (lower tray) is empty or paper is not detected.
fullDescription: Support Code 1003 specifically indicates the paper cassette (lower paper tray) is empty or cannot detect paper. This is different from Support Code 1000 which covers the rear tray. Models with both a rear tray and cassette can generate different codes for each source.

causes.cause: Paper not positioned correctly in cassette

symptoms: Printer prompts to load paper in cassette
Prints from rear tray work but cassette fails

fixSteps.title: Load Paper in Cassette
fixSteps.instruction: Pull out the paper cassette, load paper, and adjust the guides to fit the paper size.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the Cassette
fixSteps.instruction: Push the cassette fully into the printer until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Check Paper Position
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure paper is pushed against the rear edge and below the maximum fill line.

fixSteps.title: Press Resume
fixSteps.instruction: Press Resume after loading paper to clear the error.

whenToCallPro: Only if the cassette sensor is defective.

faq.q: What is the difference between the rear tray and cassette?
faq.a: The rear tray is the open tray at the top/back where you can load various paper types. The cassette is the enclosed tray that slides out from the front/bottom and is typically used for plain paper.

faq.q: Can I print from the rear tray instead?
faq.a: Yes, select the rear tray as the paper source in your print settings to bypass the cassette.

faq.q: How much paper can the cassette hold?
faq.a: Most Canon cassettes hold 100-250 sheets of plain paper depending on the model.
